description_long: This is a large, rectangular wooden crate with visible horizontal planks and reinforced vertical beams on its sides. The top of the box is open, revealing a densely packed layer of ripe red tomatoes with green stems, filling the entire interior. The wood appears weathered and natural, with knots and grain patterns visible, suggesting it is designed for durability and outdoor use. The crate is robust and utilitarian, likely used for transporting or storing produce in urban markets or street-side stalls. There are no visible handles, locks, or labeling areas, emphasizing its simple, functional design. The overall construction suggests it is intended to be placed on the ground or stacked with similar crates in a storage or market environment.
description: A large, open-topped wooden crate filled with ripe red tomatoes, featuring sturdy plank construction and visible wood grain. Designed for robust storage or transport in urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing horizontal wooden planks with visible knots and a full top layer of tomatoes.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting vertical reinforcing beams and the open top filled with tomatoes.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with similar horizontal planks and visible tomato tops.
description_view_3: Alternate side view with vertical beams and the open top densely packed with tomatoes.
